description: Prioritized validation matrix for the browser 2D-FDTD simulator.

smoke.purpose: Fast guard against load failures, non-finite fields, and broken presets.

physics.purpose: Numerical/physical validation intended for manual or scripted convergence runs.

convergence.purpose: Grid refinement for results used in teaching notes or publications.

checks: loads without console errors
step counter advances
max field remains finite
energy remains finite
cases.rationale: Baseline homogeneous Yee propagation. If this fails, no higher-level validation is meaningful.

checks: finite fields
R and T are bounded
R plus T is close to unity for low-loss media
compare R against normal-incidence Fresnel reference
cases.rationale: First quantitative check for interface monitors and material impedance.

checks: angle sweep runs
TM reflectance has a local minimum
minimum angle is near arctan(n2/n1)
cases.rationale: Validates oblique incidence, polarization handling, and sweep diagnostics.

checks: pulse leaves the domain
late-time residual energy decreases
left/right monitor reflection remains small
CPML uses graded sigma, kappa, alpha and recursive psi memory
cases.rationale: CPML quality controls every open-domain example.

checks: finite fields
guided flux is positive
field energy is concentrated near the guide
modal source launches predominantly forward with low cladding radiation
cases.rationale: Core guided-optics workflow for integrated photonics examples.

checks: finite fields after transient warmup
integrated Ez^2 is sampled in the two output-guide cores
upper/lower output field-energy split is close to 50/50
cases.rationale: Checks that the MMI scene behaves as a balanced 1x2 splitter rather than only drawing a multimode waveguide.

checks: analysis trace fills
energy envelope decays
Q proxy is finite and positive
cases.rationale: Checks spectral/ringdown post-processing used by resonator teaching scenes.

checks: ADE material loads
finite fields after several steps
active media diagnostics report ADE
cases.rationale: Fast guard for dispersive material state arrays and diagnostics.

checks: finite transmitted field behind the aperture
downstream diffraction profile is approximately symmetric about the optical axis
central diffraction lobe is stronger than the mean profile level
cases.rationale: Turns the single-slit scene from a visual aperture into a reproducible diffraction-profile check.

checks: finite transmitted field behind the two apertures
downstream profile remains approximately mirror-symmetric
profile contains multiple resolved maxima rather than one broad leak
cases.rationale: Checks that the double-slit example behaves as an interference demonstration, not merely as two holes in a PEC wall.

checks: Drude material flag and model are active
dispersive cell mask is populated
ADE polarization/current state responds to the incident field
cases.rationale: Adds a dynamic ADE-state validation beyond the fast active-media smoke label.

checks: conductive half-space cells are present
J = sigma E update path is active
field energy enters the conductor and the pulse decays at late time
cases.rationale: Guards the finite-conductivity scene as a skin-depth/attenuation example rather than only a colored slab.

checks: Lorentz ADE cells are present
effective permittivity is elevated near the configured resonance
fields remain finite while the Lorentz slab is active
cases.rationale: Checks that the Lorentz scene uses the resonant ADE branch and produces a finite dispersive-medium interaction.

checks: Debye ADE cells are present
effective permittivity exceeds the high-frequency baseline
fields remain finite while the Debye slab is active
cases.rationale: Checks that the Debye relaxation example is wired to the Debye ADE branch with a physically distinct epsilon response.

checks: plasma ADE cells are present
effective permittivity is below zero at the diagnostic frequency
fields remain finite while the plasma slab is active
cases.rationale: Checks the plasma-cutoff scene as a negative-effective-epsilon ADE medium at the teaching frequency.

checks: Drude ENZ slab cells are present
effective permittivity is close to zero at the diagnostic frequency
fields remain finite while the thin ENZ slab is active
cases.rationale: Guards the ENZ teaching example by checking the actual effective epsilon tuning rather than only the scene label.

checks: anisotropic eps_x/eps_y block cells are present
tensor diagnostics inspect the anisotropic cells
localized source deposits finite energy inside the block
cases.rationale: Checks the anisotropic-medium scene as a real tensor-epsilon block with finite field interaction.

checks: passive Drude tensor proxy block cells are present
effective tensor eigenvalues are indefinite near the source frequency
point-dipole source produces finite energy without nondispersive negative-epsilon blow-up
cases.rationale: Keeps the hyperbolic scene honest as a reduced passive Drude tensor proxy, not a causal broadband-material validation.

checks: Hz six-field bianisotropy path is active
kappa is passivity-limited and nonzero
cross-field energy remains finite inside the chiral block
cases.rationale: Adds an executable six-field/chiral response check while leaving calibrated optical-activity validation as future work.

checks: Hz six-field bianisotropy path is active
anisotropic eps/mu block has nonzero kappa
cross-field energy and passivity diagnostics remain finite
cases.rationale: Checks that the bianisotropic block exercises the intended six-field coupling rather than a scalar fallback.

checks: Hz solver is active for the gyrotropic tensor example
antisymmetric epsilon tensor cells are present
field energy overlaps the gyrotropic block
cases.rationale: Guards the qualitative gyrotropic scene as an actual tensor-route example with finite field interaction.

checks: the stack contains many alternating quarter-wave material segments
line-monitor reflectance is measurably above a weak-interface baseline
transmitted-side field energy is not dominant over the multilayer stack
cases.rationale: Covers the Bragg-stack teaching scene as a periodic dielectric mirror rather than only validating the earlier Bragg-mirror alias.

checks: the scene builds a two-dimensional high-index rod lattice
the Bloch-k sweep and reduced plane-wave estimate are active
the reduced band estimate reports finite bands and a nonzero gap proxy
cases.rationale: Checks that the photonic-crystal example is an actual periodic lattice with an executable Bloch/PWE teaching observable.

checks: the central rod is removed from an otherwise periodic lattice
a point dipole excites the defect region
the Bloch-k reduced estimate remains available for the surrounding lattice
cases.rationale: Turns the point-defect PhC scene into a runnable defect-cavity sanity check while keeping the observable at teaching-proxy level.

checks: the central lattice row is removed to create a line defect
adjacent PhC rows remain populated
finite field energy propagates in the line-defect channel
cases.rationale: Checks that the PhC waveguide is a real line-defect guide, not a full lattice or an empty channel without excitation.

checks: an L3-like central defect is present
nearby holes are shifted or resized through the optimized-cavity geometry
analysis and Bloch/PWE proxies remain finite
cases.rationale: Guards the optimized PhC cavity as a geometry-plus-analysis proxy without implying publication-grade Q extraction.

checks: a high-index PhC rod lattice is present
rod centroids are measurably displaced from the ideal lattice
finite field energy overlaps the disordered lattice
cases.rationale: Checks that the disorder example is not a perfect PhC clone and keeps the validation to geometry and finite interaction.

checks: the central defect is excited by two opposite-phase dipoles
the lattice remains nearly mirror symmetric
analysis produces a finite reduced leakage/Q proxy
cases.rationale: Validates the dark-mode scene as a symmetry-controlled defect proxy rather than a generic two-source animation.

checks: the central defect is retained
source amplitudes/phases deliberately break perfect dark-mode symmetry
the reduced PhC estimate reports finite asymmetry and Q/leakage proxies
cases.rationale: Ensures the quasi-BIC is intentionally perturbed from the symmetry-protected case while staying numerically finite.

checks: an L3-like PhC defect is driven with opposite-phase symmetric dipoles
the Bloch-k sweep and reduced PWE path are active
the reduced Q/leakage proxy remains finite under short browser execution
cases.rationale: Checks the symmetry-protected BIC as a reduced PhC-cavity workflow with explicit symmetry and analysis observables.

checks: a high-index bus waveguide is present
a side-coupled resonator is separated by an air gap
finite field energy reaches the resonator region during the run
cases.rationale: Guards the Fano resonator scene as an actual bus-plus-side-resonator coupling example rather than a disconnected geometry sketch.

checks: the SSH chain contains the expected number of high-index sites
the first two gaps follow the trivial d1 > d2 dimerization
the analytic SSH Bloch reference reports winding zero and a finite gap
cases.rationale: Guards the trivial SSH scene against inverted dimerization or loss of the analytic Bloch reference.

checks: the SSH chain contains the expected number of high-index sites
the first two gaps follow the topological d1 < d2 dimerization
the analytic SSH Bloch reference reports winding one and a finite gap
cases.rationale: Checks that the topological SSH scene is not accidentally identical to the trivial dimerization.

checks: the interface chain keeps the high-index SSH site pattern
the analytic reduced model marks an edge-state interface
a central dipole deposits measurable energy near the interface
cases.rationale: Turns the SSH interface into an executable edge-proxy check without claiming a full eigenmode solve.

checks: the disordered SSH interface keeps a finite high-index site chain
analysis samples and clean-limit SSH Bloch reference remain available
the reduced model still marks the edge-state proxy
cases.rationale: Checks the disordered SSH scene as a robust qualitative edge-interface example with an explicit clean-limit reference.

checks: the non-Hermitian SSH chain has alternating gain/loss material cells
saturable gain stabilization is active
the reduced non-Hermitian SSH gap proxy remains finite
cases.rationale: Guards the non-Hermitian SSH scene as a balanced gain/loss proxy with finite reduced topology metrics.

checks: the honeycomb-like dimer lattice has many high-index sublattice rods
both upper and lower halves share the same sublattice mass bias
finite field energy overlaps the lattice
cases.rationale: Checks the baseline honeycomb geometry before validating domain-wall variants.

checks: upper and lower honeycomb domains have inverted sublattice bias
the poynting/scalar view used for the teaching scene remains active
field energy is present along the straight interface channel
cases.rationale: Validates the Valley-Hall scene as an inverted-domain interface, not merely a generic honeycomb array.

checks: the left side retains a horizontal Valley-Hall domain wall
the right side turns into a vertical-domain segment
the bent channel carries finite field energy in the reduced check
cases.rationale: Checks that the bend example changes the domain-wall geometry rather than reusing the straight interface.

checks: the SSH chain is materially modulated
analysis samples are collected for the qualitative pumping proxy
finite field energy is present near the launched excitation
cases.rationale: Keeps the Thouless-pump-like scene honest as a modulated qualitative proxy rather than an unmodulated SSH chain.

checks: the Valley-Hall inverted-domain geometry remains present
a strong PEC defect occupies the channel region
finite field energy reaches the defect/channel region
cases.rationale: Checks that the topology-with-defect scene contains both the domain wall and the intended strong scatterer.

checks: coating and substrate material segments are present
line-monitor reflectance remains bounded for the coated interface
field energy overlaps the multilayer region during the run
cases.rationale: Provides an executable sanity check that the quarter-wave coating scene is a multilayer antireflection example, not just a bare interface.

checks: many alternating material segments are present
line-monitor reflectance is measurably above a weak-interface baseline
transmitted-side field energy is not dominant over the stack energy
cases.rationale: Checks that the Bragg mirror behaves as a reflective periodic stack at the teaching level.

checks: high-index incident medium terminates at an air interface
interface band stores measurable evanescent/near-field energy
far-side air energy remains bounded relative to interface energy
cases.rationale: Turns total internal reflection into a measurable evanescent-field scene instead of relying only on the field snapshot.

checks: second high-index medium is present beyond the air gap
interface/gap band stores measurable evanescent energy
far-side high-index region receives finite tunneled field energy
cases.rationale: Checks the qualitative tunneling contrast expected from the frustrated-TIR example.

checks: two Bragg mirrors and central cavity material are present
cavity stores a measurable fraction of the field energy
mirror regions carry finite energy during excitation
cases.rationale: Adds a cavity-localization guard for the Fabry-Perot example without claiming a calibrated Q or linewidth.

checks: analysis trace fills for the driven cavity
central cavity line shows standing-wave contrast
Bragg mirror material cells are present
cases.rationale: Checks that the standing-wave Fabry-Perot scene produces a measurable intracavity pattern.

checks: ring and bus waveguide high-index cells are present
ring stores measurable field energy
ring/bus energy ratio is finite during modal excitation
cases.rationale: Adds a minimal ring-coupling observable before more rigorous resonance-spectrum validation.

checks: ring, bus, and drop waveguide cells are present
ring stores measurable field energy
drop bus receives finite coupled energy
cases.rationale: Checks that the add-drop ring example includes both ports and produces a finite coupled-field proxy.

checks: PEC cylinder cells are present
the incident field reaches the scatterer region
central downstream energy is bounded relative to the upstream incident band
cases.rationale: Guards the PEC-cylinder scene as a real obstacle/scatterer instead of an empty plane-wave case.

checks: dielectric cylinder cells are present
field energy overlaps the cylinder
off-axis scattered energy is finite
cases.rationale: Checks that the dielectric-cylinder scattering example has both geometry and a measurable scattered-field proxy.

checks: high-index cylinder cells are present
the broadband pulse excites energy in the scatterer region
near-field scattering outside the forward channel is finite
cases.rationale: Provides a first executable guard for the Mie-like high-index cylinder without claiming a calibrated 2D Mie spectrum.

checks: PEC cylinder cells are present
analysis samples accumulate
the scattering-width/NTFF proxy is finite and nonzero
cases.rationale: Turns the RCS cylinder into an executable NTFF/scattering-width sanity check.

checks: lossy cylinder cells are present
field energy reaches the lossy region
downstream channel energy is bounded by absorption and scattering
cases.rationale: Checks that the lossy-cylinder scene actually contains a lossy absorber interacting with the field.

checks: two high-index scatterers are present as a dimer
the gap carries finite near-field energy
off-axis scattering remains finite
cases.rationale: Guards the dielectric dimer as a coupled near-field example rather than two inert visual disks.

checks: Hz polarization is active for the Kerker-like example
analysis samples accumulate
forward/backward scattering proxy is finite and asymmetric
cases.rationale: Checks the reduced forward/backward scattering observable used by the Kerker-like teaching scene.

checks: deterministic scatterer cluster is present
field energy spreads outside the central incident channel
transmitted channel remains finite
cases.rationale: Adds an executable multiple-scattering spread check without claiming a diffusion coefficient.

checks: many weak disorder inclusions are present
lateral speckle-like energy is finite
forward channel is not empty
cases.rationale: Guards the weak-localization scene as a disorder/spread example, not a calibrated coherent-backscattering validation.

checks: dense high-contrast disorder is present
analysis samples accumulate
energy remains trapped/scattered before the right boundary
cases.rationale: Adds a conservative localization/trapping proxy for the dense-disorder example; calibrated localization length remains future work.

checks: extended weak random medium is present
analysis samples accumulate
lateral spread and finite forward transport are measurable
cases.rationale: Checks the diffusive-random-medium example for finite spread and transport without claiming a fitted mean free path.

checks: scene uses Hz polarization and Drude metal
surface band around the metal-dielectric interface carries measurable energy
surface energy exceeds far-air and bulk-metal comparison bands
cases.rationale: Checks that the SPP interface scene actually excites an interface-localized field component.

checks: scene uses Hz polarization and Drude metal
grating teeth are present above the base metal interface
surface band around the grating/interface carries measurable energy
cases.rationale: Guards the plasmonic grating contract after the SPP scene fix: a shallow periodic Drude grating should remain present and surface-coupled.

checks: a Drude disk is present at the expected localized-plasmon position
field energy overlaps the dispersive nanodisk
near-field energy around the disk remains finite
cases.rationale: Checks the localized plasmon scene as an actual Drude nanodisk with finite field coupling.

checks: two Drude disks are present
the narrow gap between disks is geometrically open
the gap stores finite field energy during the run
cases.rationale: Guards the plasmonic dimer as a two-particle hot-gap example instead of a single merged object.

checks: the dielectric bar array is present
bar heights span a clear phase-gradient range
field energy overlaps the metasurface column
cases.rationale: Checks the metasurface phase-gradient teaching scene as a real graded bar array.

checks: a graded lossy sheet is backed by a PEC layer
line diagnostics collect finite samples
the reduced absorption proxy is finite
cases.rationale: Makes the perfect-absorber scene executable as a lossy-sheet plus backplane sanity check, not a visual-only stack.

checks: analysis metrics are populated
effective epsilon and mu form a double-negative band at the diagnostic frequency
in-slab phase-front proxy is finite and coherent enough for a teaching observable
cases.rationale: Makes the negative-index scene execute its reduced observable path rather than relying only on material flags.

checks: analysis metrics are populated
effective epsilon and mu form a double-negative band at the diagnostic frequency
image-plane transfer proxy is finite and nonzero
cases.rationale: Adds a concrete image-transfer sanity check for the qualitative superlens analogue.

checks: the Hz solver and Drude tensor route are active
the annular hyperlens contains tensor-like dispersive material
the reduced ring-sampling MTF proxy produces finite samples
cases.rationale: Turns the hyperlens tracking gap into an executable reduced-MTF proxy while preserving the caveat that it is a 2D analogue.

checks: a Drude ENZ slab is present near the dipole
the diagnostic effective permittivity is near-zero in magnitude
field energy overlaps the ENZ material region
cases.rationale: Checks the ENZ emitter scene as a real near-dipole dispersive slab with finite overlap.

checks: Kerr nonlinear material is enabled
the nonlinear slab contains many active cells
field energy overlaps the slab after the wave reaches it
cases.rationale: Guards the Kerr slab as an active nonlinear-material scene rather than a passive dielectric slab.

checks: chi2 harmonic material is enabled
analysis samples accumulate
the reduced second-harmonic proxy is finite
cases.rationale: Checks that the SHG scene runs through the harmonic route and reports a finite H2 teaching proxy.

checks: chi3 harmonic material is enabled
analysis samples accumulate
the reduced third-harmonic proxy is finite
cases.rationale: Checks that the THG scene runs through the harmonic route and reports a finite H3 teaching proxy.

checks: Kerr nonlinear guide/slab is enabled
analysis samples accumulate
the sideband/broadening proxy is finite
cases.rationale: Turns the SPM Kerr pulse scene into an executable reduced spectral-broadening check.

checks: Kerr nonlinear cavity material is present
mode-launched guide energy reaches the cavity/active section
analysis samples accumulate for the qualitative bistability proxy
cases.rationale: Checks the Kerr bistable cavity as a nonlinear resonator geometry with finite active-section overlap.

checks: phase-change material is enabled
the VO2-like slab contains many phase-state cells
the phase-state proxy responds during the run
cases.rationale: Checks the VO2 switching slab as a finite-threshold phase-state scene without claiming a calibrated transition curve.

checks: a compact phase-change memory cell is embedded in a guide
analysis samples accumulate
field energy overlaps the PCM cell
cases.rationale: Guards the PCM memory-cell scene as a phase-change guide device with finite field overlap.

checks: phase-state saturable absorber material is enabled
loss contrast exists between off/on state settings
the phase-state proxy responds during the run
cases.rationale: Checks the saturable absorber as an executable bleaching analogue with active phase-state cells.

checks: Kerr and phase-change routes are both enabled
the active control section contains nonlinear/phase-change cells
mode-launched energy reaches the active region and output arms
cases.rationale: Checks the all-optical switch as a mode-launched active-section workflow, not just a passive guide drawing.

checks: phase-state lossy limiter material is enabled
the limiter has a strong high-intensity loss state
field energy overlaps the limiter section
cases.rationale: Checks the nonlinear limiter as a finite active-loss section with reduced phase-state observables.

checks: exported scene snapshot is serializable
snapshot includes grid dimensions
snapshot includes drawn material cells
cases.rationale: Ensures examples can be reproduced across sessions.

checks: evanescent source stores explicit k_parallel/k0 > 1
evanescent injection applies tangential phase and normal exponential decay
in-plane electric dipole preset forces the Hz solver and injects localized Jx/Jy
progressive dipole array phase step is derived from the target steering angle
compare source patterns against analytic phase and envelope references
cases.rationale: Tracks Atlas 11, 24, and 32 after replacing ambiguous source parameters with explicit physics metadata.

checks: line monitors report finite separated incident, reflected, and transmitted powers
oblique-incidence R/T does not rely on raw transverse field averaging
TE/TM angular sweep stores both numerical polarizations
Fresnel/Brewster reference curve is available for comparison
NTFF/scattering scenes expose normalized angular observables
cases.rationale: Tracks the shared monitor and sweep infrastructure used by Atlas 9, 10, 15, 16, 34, 62, 65, and related observable-heavy scenes.

checks: Kerr, phase-change, harmonic, and saturable-gain responses use physical field scale after internal renormalization
SHG/THG scenes report harmonic proxies H2/H3
SPM and bistability amplitude sweeps report sideband and hysteresis proxies
VO2/PCM/saturable/limiter scenes report average switched-state proxy
PT/EP scenes report gamma-sweep modal splitting proxy
cases.rationale: Tracks Atlas 111-120 and 131-132 after making nonlinear intensity, harmonic polarization, phase-state thresholds, and saturable gain consistent with field renormalization.

checks: finite fields
measure in-slab beam-centroid angle
measure in-slab field-gradient phase-front proxy and coherence
measure superlens image transfer and image width
beam-case power-balance residual
grid convergence
cases.rationale: Tracks the quantitative validation gap for double-negative and superlens scenes after first-pass field observables.

checks: finite fields
accumulate input and output monitor DFT phasors
estimate reduced S_m0 transmitted and reflected sidebands
report measured-order power-balance residual
report modulation phase coherence to distinguish uniform temporal modulation from traveling or staggered phase modulation
de-embed reference planes
repeat with reverse and sideband incident channels
cases.rationale: Tracks the calibrated Floquet scattering-matrix validation gap after adding a normalized measured-order power residual and a phase-coherence check to the reduced S_m0 readout.

checks: non-Hermitian skin analogue reports field centroid, edge accumulation, and gain/loss localization proxies
BIC + Kerr and BIC + ENZ report active-material overlap alongside Bloch-k leakage proxy
Janus/topological and Huygens/cavity workflows report guide, cavity, source-overlap, and material-energy proxies
topology + temporal modulation, nonreciprocal Valley Hall, and space-time crystal expose coupled workflow proxies together with reduced Floquet sideband readouts
metrics remain finite during short browser execution

acceptance.status: first-coupled-workflow-proxy-pass; calibrated eigenmode, non-Bloch, and device-level validation pending

cases.rationale: Tracks Atlas 133-140 after adding scene-level reduced observables for composite workflows rather than relying only on reused component diagnostics.

checks: finite fields
measure inner and outer ring field transfer
measure normalized angular-harmonic MTF proxy
estimate MTF50 angular order
compare against controlled subwavelength source pattern
grid convergence
cases.rationale: Tracks the hyperlens PSF/MTF validation gap after adding angular-harmonic transfer metrics from FDTD field samples.

checks: six-field path finite
cross-polarized energy finite
output-line cross-polarized conversion finite
output-line primary/cross correlation and kappa-signed convention proxy finite
passivity margin positive
compare against calibrated omega/chiral reference
grid convergence
cases.rationale: Tracks the quantitative validation gap for six-field bianisotropy after adding direct conversion and passivity-margin observables.
